subject: Re: improving file read/write
body:
For K2 we were told that superresolution data 1) had an advantage DQE-wise, 2) tile-based MotionCor2 would suffer less from interpolation errors and 3) 32 bit float is the best way to go. We all did it until Rado compared superresolution data binned vs Fourier-cropped and there was very little difference. We dropped argument 1 and 2, most packages still use 32 but float anyway.

SerialEM can save 16 bit integer of scaled 32 bit float data, theoretically less good, but it has not been tested yet what the true difference is.

For K3 I guess we await all such tests and then decide how conservative we want to be vs storage and throughput requirements.

Best,

Wim

> On 10. Apr 2019, at 19:43, Dimitry Tegunov <tegunov@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Ah, sorry, I didn't realize you were talking about superres data. Given it's 6x as much data as the movies I tested with, the 40s figure seems accurate. 7 seconds is also about as long as IMOD takes to read it on my machine, and that's without the additional preprocessing Warp does, so I don't think there is much to be gained through optimization.
> 
> For K2 images, superres provides a marginally higher DQE. Is it worth using on the K3? Did you set binning to 1x in the import settings? That would at least make everything else faster.
> 
> The next release will allow you to run multiple processes per GPU if there is enough memory, this way you can amortize some of the IO costs.
> 
> It looks like we're not quite ready for K3 superres yet, sorry :-(
> 
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
> 
>> On Wednesday, April 10, 2019 at 6:15:33 PM UTC+2, Reza B wrote:
>> Hmm, that's odd. the movies are in TIFF, about 607 MB per file, 11520x8184 pixels. any ideas where I can look for sources of problem?
>> Reza
>> 
>>> On Tuesday, April 9, 2019 at 1:03:25 PM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>> Hi Reza,
>>> 
>>> what file format are the movies in? I'm getting 7.3 s for reading a 40 frame K3 movie compressed as TIFF off a single SSD.
>>> 
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>> 
>>>> On Tuesday, April 9, 2019 at 6:36:05 PM UTC+2, Reza B wrote:
>>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>> 
>>>> I was trying Warp on our first batch of K3 data. There are 60 frames as opposed to 40-50 which I used to have and of course the area is abotu 50% larger. The processing speed has come down to ~25 hour and it's all in file read write, about 40s for read, 23 s for write, other steps take 2-3 seconds. The data is on 4 striped SSDs with 64 GB RAM and a 6 core Intel i7-6850 3.6 GHz. I was wondering if there's anything that can be optimized further to improve overall read write speeds. Thanks.
>>>> 
>>>> Best,
>>>> Reza
> 
> --
